title: What To Expect From Upstart's Q1 Earnings
text: Analysts will be monitoring several key areas for UPST's Q1 earnings: (1) progress in improving margins for auto and home loan products, (2) the pace at which operating expenses moderate and profitability improves, and (3) milestones in the regulatory review process for Upstart's national bank charter application. Key Expectations: Before Q1 earnings, the market was expecting Upstart's revenue to grow 43.1% year-on-year. Sell-side analysts expected revenue to grow 33.4% over the next 12 months, a deceleration versus the last two years. Forward Guidance: The company maintained its revenue guidance for the full year at $1.4 billion, up 34.5% from 2025, and adjusted EBITDA of $294 million, up 28%. Management confirmed that profitability was temporarily impacted by front-loaded expenses, product mix, and seasonality, but projected sequential margin improvement through 2026. Regulatory Focus: The national bank charter application is an important milestone that could unlock new strategic opportunities for the company. Historical Performance: Over the last 1 year, UPST had beaten EPS estimates 100% of the time and beaten revenue estimates 100% of the time, suggesting a strong track record of execution.
